content.problemStatement:
A string is almost palindromic if we can rearrange its characters in such a way that it becomes a palindrome. A positive integer is almost palindromic if its decimal representation without leading zeros is an almost palindromic string.

You are given two integers LLL and RRR. Find the number of almost palindromic integers between LLL and RRR (inclusive). Since this number could be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: the sum of lengths of RRR over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR (without leading zeros).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of almost palindromic integers modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
